candidates.label: Individual Debtor
candidates.definition: An individual who owes money or other obligations to another party and is personally responsible for repayment.

candidates.label: Personal Injury and Tort Law
candidates.definition: Law which allows an injured person to obtain compensation from the person who caused the injury.
synonyms: Derecho de Daños y Perjuicios Personales
Direito de danos pessoais e responsabilidade civil
Droit des dommages corporels et des délits civils
人身傷害および不法行為法

candidates.label: Individual Rights Law
candidates.definition: Individual rights such as privacy and the freedom of thought, speech, religion, press, assembly, and movement.

candidates.label: Injury
candidates.definition: An injury refers to damage or harm caused to the structure or function of the body, either due to external forces or internal causes. Injuries can be physical or emotional and can result from accidents, falls, hits, weapons, and other causes, leading to pain, discomfort, or disability.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Ruleset
candidates.definition: A Personal Injury Ruleset is a set of legal rules and guidelines that specifically pertain to cases involving physical or emotional harm caused by another party's negligence or intentional actions.
synonyms: Conjunto de regras de lesões pessoais
Ensemble de règles sur les dommages corporels
人身傷害ルールセット

candidates.label: Contusion
candidates.definition: Moderate traumatic brain injury (TBI) is a disruption in brain function caused by an external mechanical force, leading to a loss of consciousness lasting from 30 minutes to 24 hours, or post-traumatic amnesia lasting from 1 to 7 days. It requires medical intervention and often results in lasting cognitive, physical, and emotional impairments that may necessitate rehabilitation.

candidates.label: Concussion
candidates.definition: Mild traumatic brain injury (concussion) is a form of TBI resulting from a blow or jolt to the head that temporarily disrupts brain function. Symptoms can include headache, confusion, dizziness, and memory loss, and while typically short-term, they may require rest and medical monitoring.

candidates.label: Individual Person
candidates.definition: An individual or natural person is a person that is an individual human being, as opposed to a legal person or entity, which may be a private or public organization.

candidates.label: Personal Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Personal Injury Claims are civil claims or tort claims seeking compensation for physical or emotional harm caused by the negligence or intentional actions of another party, including but not limited to slip-and-fall accidents, medical malpractice, and motor vehicle accidents.

candidates.label: Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury
candidates.definition: Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury: A claim for damages resulting from a personal injury that has not yet manifested, but is likely to occur in the future as a direct result of the initial injury.
synonyms: Blessure future résultant d'un préjudice corporel actuel
Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury
Lesiones futuras a partir de una lesión personal ilícita presente
Lesão futura decorrente de lesão pessoal ilícita presente
Zukünftige Verletzung aus gegenwärtiger unerlaubter Handlung
פציעה עתידית מפגיעה אישית עוינת נוכחית
वर्तमान अनैतिक व्यक्तिगत घायली से भविष्य की घायली
现行侵权人身伤害的未来伤害
現在の不法行為による将来の傷害

hierarchy_path.label: Future Injury from Present Tortious Personal Injury

candidates.label: Industrial Injury Claims
candidates.definition: Industrial Injury Claims refer to legal claims made by individuals who have suffered harm or injury while working in an industrial setting, such as a factory or construction site.
synonyms: Reivindicações de indenização por acidentes de trabalho

candidates.label: Education Liability
candidates.definition: Education Liability refers to the legal responsibility and accountability of educational institutions and their staff for any harm or injury caused to students or their families, including civil claims related to privacy of school records, corporal punishment, compulsory education, and prayer in public school.

candidates.label: Construction Liability
candidates.definition: Construction Liability refers to the legal responsibility of parties involved in construction projects for damages or injuries resulting from their actions or inactions, including claims related to construction defects or negligence.

candidates.label: Family Liability
candidates.definition: Family Liability refers to the legal responsibility of family members for the actions or debts of other family members, including spousal and parent-child liability, as well as agency and vicarious liability.

candidates.label: Spousal Liability
candidates.definition: Spousal Liability refers to the legal responsibility of one spouse for the debts incurred by the other spouse.

candidates.label: Agent Liability
candidates.definition: Agent Liability refers to the legal responsibility of an individual or entity acting as an agent for another party, for any harm caused to a third party due to their negligence or breach of fiduciary duty.

candidates.label: Partnership Liability for Injury
candidates.definition: Partnership Liability for Injury refers to the legal responsibility of a partnership for injuries caused to a third party due to the actions or negligence of the partnership or its partners.
synonyms: Responsabilidad de la sociedad por lesiones
Responsabilité de la société de personnes pour blessures
घोषणा द्वारा घात के लिए भागीदारी दायित्व
パートナーシップの責任における傷害

candidates.label: Liability for Injury to Pets
candidates.definition: Liability for Injury to Pets refers to the legal responsibility for harm caused to a domesticated animal, such as a dog or cat, and the resulting damages that may be awarded to the pet owner.
synonyms: Responsabilidade por lesões em animais de estimação
Responsabilité pour les blessures aux animaux de compagnie
पालतू जानवरों के घायल होने के लिए दायित्व
ペットの負傷に対する責任

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Law
candidates.definition: Law covering all the aspects of registering and titling motor vehicles, and licensing drivers.

candidates.label: Sport Utility Vehicle
candidates.definition: A Sport Utility Vehicle (SUV) is a type of motor vehicle designed to combine the capabilities of a passenger car with features typically found in off-road vehicles. SUVs are characterized by their larger size, higher ground clearance, and often, four-wheel-drive or all-wheel-drive systems.

candidates.label: Autonomous Vehicle
candidates.definition: An autonomous or connected vehicle, also known as a self-driving or driverless car, is a vehicle equipped with technology that allows it to navigate and operate with varying levels of human intervention (Level 1 through Level 4), or no human intervention (Level 5). Such vehicles rely on sensors, cameras, and artificial intelligence to perceive their environment and make decisions based on pre-programmed algorithms and real-time data.

candidates.label: Recreational Vehicle
candidates.definition: A recreational vehicle (RV) is a motorized or towable vehicle equipped with living accommodations, typically including sleeping, cooking, and bathroom facilities. RVs are used for travel, camping, and recreation, providing a mobile home away from home for individuals and families.

candidates.label: Off-Road Vehicle
candidates.definition: An off-road vehicle is a type of motorized vehicle specifically designed for use on rough and unpaved terrain. Examples of off-road vehicles include All-Terrain Vehicles (ATVs), commonly known as four-wheelers, and dune buggies.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Asset
candidates.definition: A Motor Vehicle is a self-propelled vehicle that runs on the surface and not on rails, primarily used for the transportation of people or goods. Types include cars, trucks, motorcycles, and buses, and they are usually powered by internal combustion engines or electric motors. This item is relevant to several areas of law (e.g., Bankruptcy).

candidates.label: Driver's License
candidates.definition: A driver's license is a legal document issued by governmental authorities that authorizes an individual to operate a motor vehicle on public roads. The license is typically granted after the individual has passed both written and practical driving tests, and it may come with restrictions based on age, type of vehicle, or other factors.

candidates.label: Commercial Motor Vehicle Indicator
candidates.definition: Y/N code identifying whether the incident occurred while the driver was operating a commercial vehicle.
synonyms: 商用車インジケーター

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry group comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components (except springs), motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmission and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, motor vehicle metal stampings, and other motor vehicle parts and accessories. This industry group includes establishments that rebuild motor vehicle parts.

candidates.label: Other Motor Vehicle Parts Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing and/or rebuilding motor vehicle parts and accessories (except motor vehicle gasoline engines and engine parts, motor vehicle electrical and electronic equipment, motor vehicle steering and suspension components, motor vehicle brake systems, motor vehicle transmissions and power train parts, motor vehicle seating and interior trim, and motor vehicle stampings).

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Metal Stamping
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in manufacturing motor vehicle stampings, such as fenders, tops, body parts, trim, and molding.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in towing light or heavy motor vehicles, both local and long-distance. These establishments may provide incidental services, such as storage and emergency road repair services.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Towing
candidates.definition: See industry description for 488410.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Negligence
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Negligence refers to a legal claim for damages resulting from the careless or reckless operation of a motor vehicle, which causes injury or harm to another person.

candidates.label: Theft from Motor Vehicle
candidates.definition: The theft of articles from a motor vehicle, whether locked or unlocked.
synonyms: Vol dans un véhicule à moteur

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability
candidates.definition: Motor Vehicle Dealership Liability refers to the legal responsibility of a car dealership for any harm or damages caused to a customer or third party as a result of the dealership's actions or negligence in the sale or maintenance of a vehicle.

candidates.label: Van (Motor Vehicle)
candidates.definition: A van is a motor vehicle typically larger than a car, designed to transport passengers or cargo. Vans come in various sizes and configurations and are commonly used for transporting goods, people, or both, depending on their purpose and design.

candidates.label: Motor Vehicle Body and Trailer Manufacturing
candidates.definition: This industry comprises establishments primarily engaged in (1) manufacturing motor vehicle bodies and cabs or (2) manufacturing truck, automobile and utility trailers, truck trailer chassis, detachable trailer bodies, and detachable trailer chassis.  The products made may be sold separately or may be assembled on purchased chassis and sold as complete vehicles.  Motor homes are units where the motor and the living quarters are contained in the same integrated unit, while travel trailers are designed to be towed by a motor unit, such as an automobile or a light truck.

candidates.label: Unemployment Benefits Law
candidates.definition: Unemployment benefits refer to those benefits given by the government to unemployed persons.
synonyms: Loi sur les allocations de chômage
חוק זכויות למען עזרה בביטוח אבטלה

candidates.label: Employee Pension and Benefits Law
candidates.definition: Law relating to retirement and welfare benefit plans, with particular emphasis on benefits arrangements.
synonyms: Droit des pensions et des avantages sociaux

candidates.label: Employee Benefits Motion
candidates.definition: A request made by a debtor in a bankruptcy case seeking authorization to continue providing employee benefits, such as health insurance and retirement plans, to its employees.
synonyms: Demande d'avantages sociaux pour les employés

candidates.label: Dental Insurance Benefits
candidates.definition: Dental insurance benefits are a specialized form of health insurance offered to employees that covers part or all of the costs associated with dental care, such as routine check-ups, cleanings, and certain dental procedures.

candidates.label: Time-Off Benefits
candidates.definition: Time-off benefits encompass policies or perks that allow employees to take time away from work for various reasons, such as vacation, illness, or personal needs, often with pay.

candidates.label: Employment Benefits
candidates.definition: Employment Benefits are additional compensations or advantages provided to employees over and above their regular salary, often as part of an employment contract. These benefits may include healthcare, retirement plans, paid time off, and other perks designed to attract and retain employees.

candidates.label: Employee Benefits Security Administration
candidates.definition: The Employee Benefits Security Administration (EBSA) is an agency of the United States Department of Labor responsible for administering, regulating and enforcing the provisions of Title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 (ERISA).
synonyms: Administración de Seguridad de Beneficios para Empleados
Administration de la sécurité des avantages sociaux des employés
Administração de Segurança de Benefícios aos Empregados

candidates.label: Stroke
candidates.definition: A vascular insult to the brain, commonly referred to as a stroke or cerebrovascular accident, occurs when blood flow to a part of the brain is disrupted, leading to tissue damage and neurological deficits. This condition can result from either ischemia (lack of blood flow) or hemorrhage (bleeding) within the brain.

candidates.label: B Corporation
candidates.definition: A B Corporation is a type of corporation that is legally required to consider the impact of its decisions not only on its shareholders, but also on its stakeholders, such as employees, customers, and the environment.

candidates.label: Public Benefits Objectives
candidates.definition: Public benefits objectives refer to legal services related to the accurate and efficient distribution of public assistance to eligible recipients. For example, underpayment objectives focus on identifying and rectifying instances where beneficiaries receive less than they are entitled to, often due to administrative errors or oversight. Overpayment objectives aim to detect and recover funds that have been erroneously disbursed in excess of eligibility, to maintain the integrity and sustainability of the benefit program.

candidates.label: Pension and Benefits Claims
candidates.definition: Pension and benefits claims are legal actions initiated by an employee against their employer for not complying with applicable laws or the terms of an employment agreement regarding retirement and other benefits. These claims can involve issues such as the mismanagement or mishandling of employee pension or retirement accounts, denial or reduction of employee benefits, or failure to provide required benefits such as health insurance or disability coverage. Employees may also bring pension and benefits claims if their employer retaliates against them for asserting their rights to these benefits.
synonyms: Réclamations de pensions et d'avantages sociaux

candidates.label: Analysis of Employee Benefits/Pensions
candidates.definition: The Analysis of Employee Benefits/Pensions is the examination and evaluation of the benefits and pension plans provided to employees by an employer, typically in the context of legal obligations.
synonyms: Analyse des avantages sociaux/retraites des employés
従業員の福利厚生/年金の分析

candidates.label: Unemployment Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Unemployment public benefits refer to government programs that provide financial assistance to individuals who have lost their jobs and are actively seeking new employment. These benefits are typically time-limited and contingent on meeting certain eligibility criteria like work history and reason for unemployment.

candidates.label: Provider of Public Benefits
candidates.definition: Provider of public benefits refers to the governmental entity responsible for administering and disbursing public assistance programs. These can be national, state, or local agencies, each offering different types of benefits tailored to the needs and eligibility criteria of their jurisdiction.

candidates.label: Home Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A home insurance policy is a contract between a homeowner and an insurance provider that offers financial protection against damages to the home, its contents, or liability claims arising from injuries on the property. The policy outlines the covered perils, such as fire, theft, or natural disasters, and requires the payment of premiums for the coverage to remain active.

candidates.label: Homeowner's Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: A homeowner's insurance policy is a contract between a homeowner and an insurance provider that offers financial protection against damages to the home, its contents, and liability claims arising from injuries on the property. The policy typically covers a range of perils such as fire, theft, and natural disasters. Premiums are usually required for the coverage to remain active.

candidates.label: Insurance Form
candidates.definition: An Insurance Form is a standardized document used by an insurance provider for capturing relevant information to evaluate, administer, or process an insurance policy. This could be for applications, claims, renewals, or any other insurance-related activities.

candidates.label: Universal Life Insurance
candidates.definition: Universal life insurance is a type of permanent life insurance that offers both a death benefit and a cash value component, similar to whole life insurance. However, it differs in its flexibility, allowing policyholders to adjust the premiums and death benefit within certain limits, and it often has an investment element where the cash value can be invested for potential growth.

candidates.label: Statement of Good Faith
candidates.definition: A Statement of Good Faith is a written declaration made in a Bankruptcy and Restructuring Document, expressing the honest intention of the declarant to act in good faith towards the other parties involved in the proceedings.

candidates.label: Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: An insurance policy is a contract between an insurance provider and a policyholder that outlines the terms and conditions under which the insurer will provide coverage and financial compensation for specific risks, events, or damages. The policy specifies the premiums to be paid by the policyholder, the coverage limits, and the obligations of each party in the event of a claim.

candidates.label: Insurance Deductible Amount
candidates.definition: An insurance deductible amount, in the context of legal matters, is the portion of a covered loss that the insured party is responsible for paying before the insurance company's coverage begins.

candidates.label: Religious Market Segment
candidates.definition: The religious market segment refers to a division of the market that is specifically targeted towards consumers based on their religious beliefs and practices. This segment encompasses products, services, and marketing strategies that are designed to cater to the needs, preferences, and interests of individuals who follow specific religious faiths, taking into account factors such as religious observances, dietary restrictions, and spiritual needs.

candidates.label: Insurance Carrier Bad Faith
candidates.definition: The concept of Insurance Carrier Bad Faith refers to the intentional or reckless failure of an insurance company to fulfill its contractual obligations to its policyholders, such as denying a valid claim or failing to investigate a claim in a timely manner.

candidates.label: Insurance Malpractice
candidates.definition: Insurance Malpractice refers to a legal claim made against an insurance professional or company for negligence, errors, or omissions in handling an insurance claim.
synonyms: बीमा लोगों के साथ अनुचित व्यवहार

candidates.label: Insurance Policy Rescission
candidates.definition: The act of canceling an insurance policy due to misrepresentation or fraud by the policyholder.

candidates.label: Good Faith Clause
candidates.definition: A Good Faith Clause is a provision in a contract that requires the parties to act honestly and fairly towards each other in their dealings, and to refrain from taking advantage of one another.

candidates.label: Insurance Policy Claim
candidates.definition: A claim made by an individual or entity to an insurance company for compensation or reimbursement for a loss or damage covered by an insurance policy.

candidates.label: Insurance Claims
candidates.definition: Insurance Claims: A legal demand made by an insured party to an insurance company for compensation or coverage for a loss or damage that is covered by the insurance policy.

candidates.label: Insurance Regulatory Practice
candidates.definition: Insurance Regulatory Practice involves representing companies, brokers, and other entities in complying with the regulatory requirements imposed by insurance regulatory authorities. This legal service includes obtaining licenses, navigating regulatory changes, and ensuring adherence to financial solvency, consumer protection, and reporting obligations.

candidates.label: Holdover Tenant
candidates.definition: A holdover tenant is an individual who remains in a rented property after the expiration of their lease or rental agreement. In such cases, the tenant continues to occupy the property without formal authorization or a renewed agreement with the landlord. Holdover tenancy situations often lead to legal actions, such as eviction proceedings, as the landlord seeks to regain possession of the property.

candidates.label: Tenant
candidates.definition: A person or entity who leases, rents, or occupies property (from a landlord).

candidates.label: Landlord Tenant Law
candidates.definition: Law that details the rights and duties of landlords and tenants
synonyms: Direito do locador e do locatário

candidates.label: General Business Liability Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: General Business Liability Insurance, also known simply as general liability insurance, provides coverage for common business risks, including bodily injury and property damage that occur at the business premises or as a result of business operations. It also often covers legal costs related to these risks. This type of insurance is foundational for most businesses.

candidates.label: Liability Auto Insurance Coverage Policy
candidates.definition: Liability auto insurance provides coverage for bodily injury and property damage inflicted on others in an accident for which the insured is at fault. It does not cover damages to the insured's own vehicle or medical expenses for the insured. This type of coverage is generally mandatory in most U.S. states.

candidates.label: Motion to Determine Tax Liability
candidates.definition: A Motion to Determine Tax Liability is a legal request made in a bankruptcy case to determine the amount of taxes owed by the debtor.
synonyms: Moción para determinar la responsabilidad fiscal
Moción para determinar la responsabilidad tributaria
Petição para Determinação da Responsabilidade Tributária
Requête en vue de déterminer la responsabilité fiscale
कर दायित्व निर्धारित करने के लिए याचिका
課税責任の決定を求める請願

candidates.label: Vacancy
candidates.definition: Vacancy refers to the state of a property or space being unoccupied or not in use. This can occur in both residential and commercial real estate, indicating that no tenant or occupant currently resides or operates in the property.

candidates.label: Financial Obligations
candidates.definition: Financial Obligations (Financial Liabilities) refer to any monetary commitments or liabilities an individual or entity is responsible for, such as paying off debts, fulfilling contract terms, or covering recurring expenses. These obligations can be short-term or long-term and failing to meet them could result in financial penalties or legal consequences

candidates.label: Limited Liability Partnership
candidates.definition: A limited liability partnership (LLP) is a partnership in which some or all partners (depending on the jurisdiction) have limited liabilities. It therefore can exhibit elements of partnerships and corporations. In a LLP, each partner is not responsible or liable for another partner's misconduct or negligence.

candidates.label: Property & Premises Liability Claims
candidates.definition: Property & Premises Liability Claims refer to legal claims made against a property owner or occupier for injuries or damages suffered by individuals on their property due to unsafe or hazardous conditions.
synonyms: Reclamaciones de responsabilidad de propiedad e instalaciones
Reivindicações de responsabilidade por propriedade e instalações
Réclamations pour responsabilité des biens et des locaux
財産および敷地責任請求

candidates.label: Liability to Property Invitees
candidates.definition: Liability to Property Invitees refers to the legal responsibility of a property owner or occupier to ensure the safety of individuals who are invited onto their property for business purposes, such as customers or clients.
synonyms: Haftung gegenüber eingeladenen Personen auf dem Grundstück
Responsabilidad hacia los invitados a la propiedad
Responsabilidade em relação aos convidados da propriedade
Responsabilité envers les invités sur la propriété

candidates.label: Construction Liability
candidates.definition: Construction Liability refers to the legal responsibility of parties involved in construction projects for damages or injuries resulting from their actions or inactions, including claims related to construction defects or negligence.

candidates.label: Liability Clause
candidates.definition: A Liability Clause is a provision in a contract that outlines the responsibility of one party to compensate the other party for any losses or damages incurred as a result of a breach of the contract.

candidates.label: Summer Associate
candidates.definition: A Summer Associate is a law student working in a law firm over the summer, often to assess mutual fit between the student and the firm. The experience often culminates in the firm extending an offer for a permanent position upon the student's graduation.

candidates.label: Negligence and Malpractice Law
candidates.definition: Failure to exercise the care that a reasonably prudent person or professional, in the case of professional negligence, would exercise in like circumstances and the harm caused by those actions.
synonyms: Derecho de negligencia y mala praxis
Derecho de negligencia y mala práctica
Droit de la négligence et de la faute professionnelle

candidates.label: Liability Auto Insurance Coverage Policy
candidates.definition: Liability auto insurance provides coverage for bodily injury and property damage inflicted on others in an accident for which the insured is at fault. It does not cover damages to the insured's own vehicle or medical expenses for the insured. This type of coverage is generally mandatory in most U.S. states.

candidates.label: General Business Liability Insurance Policy
candidates.definition: General Business Liability Insurance, also known simply as general liability insurance, provides coverage for common business risks, including bodily injury and property damage that occur at the business premises or as a result of business operations. It also often covers legal costs related to these risks. This type of insurance is foundational for most businesses.

candidates.label: Motion to Determine Tax Liability
candidates.definition: A Motion to Determine Tax Liability is a legal request made in a bankruptcy case to determine the amount of taxes owed by the debtor.
synonyms: Moción para determinar la responsabilidad fiscal

candidates.label: Judgment
candidates.definition: A Judgment is a written decision by a court or other tribunal determining the rights and obligations of the parties involved in a legal dispute, often following a trial or other legal proceeding.

candidates.label: Purchase Receipt
candidates.definition: A Purchase Receipt is a financial document provided by a seller to a buyer as proof of a completed transaction. It details the goods or services purchased, the amount paid, and often the method of payment.

candidates.label: Colorado Industrial Claim Appeals Office
candidates.definition: Colo. Indus. Cl. App.

candidates.label: Sumerian
